question: tell me about 369
content:
The 3-6-9 system is a widely used and straightforward method for shooting spares in bowling. Its core principle is to simplify spare conversions by maintaining a single target—Arrow 4—and making all necessary adjustments solely with your feet. The ball should be thrown straight, without any hook, to ensure predictability. Adjustments are always in increments of 3, 6, or 9 boards, making the system easy to remember and repeat under pressure.

The foundation of this system is the 'Zero Position.' This is your personal reference point on the approach from which, when aiming straight at Arrow 4, your ball consistently hits the center of Pin 1. Once identified, this Zero Position remains constant and serves as the base for all subsequent spare adjustments. The golden rule for alignment is simple: for spares on the right side of the lane, move your feet LEFT from your Zero Position; for spares on the left side of the lane, move your feet RIGHT from your Zero Position. Pins 1 and 5 are considered neutral and require no foot movement from the Zero Position.

Common mistakes bowlers make include trying to change their target, attempting to hook the ball, or making inconsistent foot adjustments. The system's effectiveness relies on the discipline of keeping the target fixed and the ball path straight.

Notice that for this spare system we do not use “inside” and “outside.” For this spare system, we use “left” and “right” instead of “inside” and “outside.” because when playing spares diagonally through the center arrow, both right‑ and left‑handed bowlers move in the same way.

Mini-drill: To find your Zero Position, stand on the approach, aim straight at Arrow 4, and throw the ball without hook. Adjust your feet until the ball consistently hits the center of Pin 1. Mentally mark this spot as your Zero Position.
